data.content: ### Project Setup and Package.json Configuration Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/step-4-generate Initializes a new project directory and creates a package.json file to manage project metadata and dependencies. This step is crucial for organizing your project and installing necessary libraries. ```shell mkdir generate-example cd generate-example ``` ```json { "name": "generate-example", "version": "1.0.0", "main": "index.mjs", "scripts": { "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1" }, "keywords": [], "author": "", "license": "ISC", "description": "" } ``` -------------------------------- ### Verify Ragie CLI Installation Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-cli Checks if the Ragie CLI is installed correctly by displaying its help message. This command should output available options and usage information upon successful installation. ```bash ragie --help ``` -------------------------------- ### Manual Ragie CLI Installation (Go) Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-cli Provides steps for manually installing the Ragie CLI by building it from source using Go. Requires Go version 1.16 or later. This involves cloning the repository and running the build command. ```bash git clone https://github.com/ragieai/ragie-cli.git cd ragie-cli go install ``` -------------------------------- ### Get Document Chunks Response Example (JSON) Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/changes-to-the-api Example JSON response from the Get Document Chunks endpoint, showing pagination details and a list of chunks. For audio/video, chunks include start/end times and media stream/download links. ```json { "pagination": { "next_cursor": null, "total_count": 10 }, "chunks": [ { "id": "id123", "index": 0, "text": "The text of the chunk.", "metadata": { "end_time": 57.68, "start_time": 2.44 }, "links": { "self": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId", "type": "application/json" }, "self_text": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId/content?media_type=text/plain-text", "type": "text/plain-text" }, "document": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/d5c36cb0-0ec4-46bd-aacc-626c252598e4", "type": "application/json" }, "document_text": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/content?media_type=text/plain-text", "type": "text/plain-text" }, "self_audio_stream": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg", "type": "audio/mpeg" }, "self_audio_download": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg&download=true", "type": "audio/mpeg" },"document_audio_stream": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg", "type": "audio/mpeg" }, "document_audio_download": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg&download=true", "type": "audio/mpeg" } } }, {"... } ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Ragie CLI via APT (Linux) Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-cli Installs the Ragie CLI tool on Linux systems that use the APT package manager. This involves downloading and executing an installation script, followed by installing the package. ```bash curl -1sLf \ 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/ragieai/ragie-repo/setup.deb.sh' \ | sudo -E bash sudo apt install ragie ``` -------------------------------- ### Import YouTube Data Example (Ragie CLI) Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-cli An example command to simulate importing data from YouTube using the Ragie CLI. The --dry-run flag prevents actual data import, showing a preview instead. ```bash ragie import youtube data/youtube.json --dry-run ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Ragie TypeScript Client Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/step-2-upload-documents Installs the official Ragie TypeScript client library using npm. This is a prerequisite for using the TypeScript SDK to interact with Ragie's APIs. ```shell npm install ragie ``` -------------------------------- ### Install OpenAI Client Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/step-4-generate Installs the official OpenAI Node.js client library using npm. This package is required to interact with OpenAI's API for language model completions. ```shell npm install openai ``` -------------------------------- ### Download Video (TypeScript) Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/example This snippet demonstrates how to get a direct download URL for a video using `chunk.self_video_download?.href`. It also includes a React component example for a button to initiate the download. ```typescript const downloadUrl = chunk.self_video_download?.href; function DownloadAudioButton({ videoUrl }: { videoUrl: string }) { return ( ); } ``` -------------------------------- ### Get Document Chunk Content Request Example Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/changes-to-the-api TypeScript example of a request to the Get Document Chunk Content endpoint. It specifies the document ID, chunk ID, and desired media type for streaming or downloading chunk content. ```typescript const response = await client.documents.getChunkContent({ documentId: "docId", chunkId: "chunkId", mediaType: "audio/mpeg", }); ``` -------------------------------- ### Get Document Chunk Response Example Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/changes-to-the-api Example JSON response for the Get Document Chunk endpoint, illustrating metadata, links, and word-level timestamps within modality_data. Note that modality_data is null for static files. ```json { "id": "id123", "index": 0, "text": "The text of the chunk.", "metadata": { "end_time": 57.68, "start_time": 2.44 }, "links": { "self": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId", "type": "application/json" }, "self_text": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId/content?media_type=text/plain-text", "type": "text/plain-text" }, "document": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId", "type": "application/json" }, "document_text": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/content?media_type=text/plain-text", "type": "text/plain-text" }, "self_audio_stream": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg", "type": "audio/mpeg" }, "self_audio_download": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg&download=true", "type": "audio/mpeg" }, "document_audio_stream": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg", "type": "audio/mpeg" }, "document_audio_download": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg&download=true", "type": "audio/mpeg" } }, "modality_data": { "type": "audio", "word_timestamps": [ { "start_time": 2.44, "end_time": 3.06, "word": " President", "probability": 0.75537109375 }, { "start_time": 3.06, "end_time": 3.38, "word": " Pitzer,", "probability": 0.67431640625 }, ... ] } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Create Instruction JSON Example Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/entity-extraction An example JSON object for creating an instruction to extract emails from a document using Ragie's API. It defines the instruction's name, activation status, scope, prompt, entity schema, and an optional filter. ```json { "name": "emails", "active": true, "scope": "chunk", "prompt": "extract all emails from the document", "entity_schema": {"type": "object","properties": {"emails": {"type": "array","items": {"type": "string"}}}}, "filter": { "type": "customer_doc" } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Ragie TypeScript SDK using npm Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-typescipt Installs the ragie npm package using the npm package manager. This is the primary method for adding the Ragie TypeScript SDK to your project. ```bash npm i ragie ``` -------------------------------- ### Generate Text using Ragie AI Retrieval and OpenAI SDK Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/example This example illustrates generating text using retrieved information from Ragie AI and OpenAI's GPT-4o model via Vercel's AI SDK. It first retrieves data using the Ragie SDK, then maps the scored chunks to a format suitable for LLM input, including text, document names, stream URLs, and timestamps. Finally, it calls `generateText` with the prompt and model to get the LLM's response. ```typescript import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai'; import { generateText } from 'ai'; const response = await ragie.retrievals.retrieve({ query: "What are the 3 main points of the presentation?", }); const sources = response.scoredChunks.map((chunk) => { return { ...chunk.documentMetadata, text: chunk.text, documentName: chunk.documentName, streamUrl: chunk.links.self_video_stream?.href, startTime: chunk.metadata?.start_time, endTime: chunk.metadata?.end_time, }; const { text } = await generateText({ model: openai('gpt-4o'), prompt: `Answer the user question using the provided sources: ${sources}`, }); console.log(text); ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Ragie TypeScript SDK using pnpm Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-typescipt Installs the ragie npm package using the pnpm package manager. This is suitable for developers who prefer pnpm for efficient package management. ```bash pnpm add ragie ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Ragie TypeScript SDK using yarn Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-typescipt Installs the ragie npm package using the yarn package manager. This command is an alternative for projects that utilize yarn for dependency management. ```bash yarn add ragie ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Ragie CLI via Homebrew (macOS) Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/ragie-cli Installs the Ragie CLI tool on macOS using the Homebrew package manager. Requires Homebrew to be installed beforehand. ```bash brew tap ragieai/tap brew install ragie ``` -------------------------------- ### Initiate Google Drive OAuth Connection with Ragie API Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/white-label-with-embed-flow This cURL command initiates the OAuth connection flow for Google Drive using a previously created authenticator. It specifies the redirect URI, partition, source type, and connection mode. The output is a JSON object containing a URL to start the authentication process. ```curl curl --location 'https://api.ragie.ai/connections/oauth' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "redirect_uri": "", "partition": "google_files", "source_type": "google_drive", "metadata": {}, "mode": "hi_res", "theme": "system", "page_limit": 100, "authenticator_id": "b5e77fb7-026c-408f-837a-2bf3b4ba0c6b" }' ``` -------------------------------- ### Create Document API Response (JSON) Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/changes-to-the-api Example JSON response from the Create Document API, indicating the status, ID, timestamps, name, and other details of the created document. ```json { "status": "partitioning", "id": "id123", "created_at": "2025-05-15T19:58:03.483172Z", "updated_at": "2025-05-15T19:58:03.586856Z", "name": "presentation.mp4", "metadata": {}, "partition": "default", "chunk_count": null, "external_id": "", "page_count": null } ``` -------------------------------- ### GET /documents Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/rate-limits Lists all documents. Rate limits vary by plan. ```APIDOC ## GET /documents ### Description Lists all documents. ### Method GET ### Endpoint /documents ### Parameters #### Path Parameters None #### Query Parameters None #### Request Body None ### Request Example ```json {} ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **documents** (array) - A list of document objects. - **document_id** (string) - The ID of the document. - **name** (string) - The name of the document. #### Response Example ```json { "documents": [ { "document_id": "doc_12345", "name": "Document 1" } ] } ``` ### Rate Limits - Developer: 1000 / m - Starter: 2000 / m - Pro: 4000 / m - Enterprise: Custom ``` -------------------------------- ### Retrievals Endpoint Response Example Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/changes-to-the-api Example JSON response for the Retrievals endpoint, showing scored chunks with text, score, ID, index, metadata (including timestamps), and document information. This endpoint supports cross-modality retrieval. ```json { "scored_chunks": [ { "text": "This is the text of a chunk", "score": 0.19090909090909092, "id": "chunkId", "index": 2, "metadata": { "end_time": 167.5, "start_time": 115.37 }, "document_id": "docId", "document_name": "presentation.mp3", "document_metadata": {}, "links": { "self": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId", "type": "application/json" }, "self_text": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId/content?media_type=text/plain-text", "type": "text/plain-text" }, "self_audio_stream": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg", "type": "audio/mpeg" }, "self_audio_download": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkid/content?media_type=audio/mpeg&download=true", "type": "audio/mpeg" } } }, ... ] } ``` -------------------------------- ### Ragie AI Connection Initialization Response (JSON) Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/initiating-a-connection This is an example of the JSON response received after successfully initializing a Ragie AI connection. It contains a 'url' field which is the redirect URL that should be used to initiate the user authentication flow. ```json {"url":"https://ragie.dev:3443/embed/connect?auth=eyJhb..."} ``` -------------------------------- ### POST /connections/oauth Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/initiating-a-connection Initiates an OAuth connection with Ragie. Generates a redirect URL to start the connection process with the specified source type. ```APIDOC ## POST /connections/oauth ### Description Initiates an OAuth connection with Ragie by generating a redirect URL. This URL allows Ragie to establish trust with your application and associate the connection with your Ragie account. ### Method POST ### Endpoint https://api.ragie.ai/connections/oauth ### Parameters #### Query Parameters - **Authorization** (string) - Required - Bearer token for authentication. #### Request Body - **source_type** (string) - Required - The type of integration to connect (e.g., "google_drive"). - **redirect_uri** (string) - Required - The URL to redirect the user back to after connection. - **metadata** (object) - Optional - Additional data to associate with the connection (e.g., {"user_id": "my-application-user-id"}). - **mode** (string) - Optional - Specifies the connection mode (e.g., "hi_res"). - **page_limit** (integer) - Optional - The maximum number of pages to sync. - **partition** (string) - Optional - An identifier for partitioning connections (e.g., "my-application-tenant-id"). ### Request Example ```json { "source_type": "google_drive", "redirect_uri": "https://example.com/link/to/your/application", "metadata": {"user_id": "my-application-user-id"}, "mode": "hi_res", "partition": "my-application-tenant-id" } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **url** (string) - The generated redirect URL for initiating the OAuth flow. ``` -------------------------------- ### Metadata Query Examples (JSON) Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/metadata-filters Illustrates how to use metadata operators and logical combinations (AND, OR) to construct effective filters for document retrieval. Covers basic equality, range checks, and array inclusion. ```json {"genre":["comedy", "documentary"]} ``` ```json {"genre":"comedy"} ``` ```json {"genre": {"$in":["documentary","action"]}} ``` ```json {"$and": [{"genre": "comedy"}, {"genre":"documentary"}]} ``` ```json {"$and": [{ "genre": { "$eq": "drama" } }, { "year": { "$gte": 2020 } }] } ``` ```json { "genre": { "$in": ["comedy", "documentary", "drama"] } } ``` ```json { "genre": { "$eq": "drama" }, "year": { "$gte": 2020 } } ``` ```json { "$or": [{ "genre": { "$eq": "drama" } }, { "year": { "$gte": 2020 } }] } ``` -------------------------------- ### Get Document Status using TypeScript Source: https://context7.com/context7/ragie_ai/llms.txt Retrieve the processing status and metadata for a specific document using its ID. The status progresses through states like 'uploading', 'processing', 'indexed', and 'ready'. The code includes an example of polling for status updates. ```typescript import { Ragie } from "ragie"; const ragie = new Ragie({ auth: "YOUR_API_KEY" }); const document = await ragie.documents.get({ documentId: "21881de1-65f7-4816-b571-3ef69661c375" }); console.log(document); // { // id: "21881de1-65f7-4816-b571-3ef69661c375", // created_at: "2024-07-05T22:21:21.099975Z", // updated_at: "2024-07-05T22:21:37.521837Z", // status: "ready", // name: "Quarterly Report", // metadata: { department: "sales", quarter: "Q4", year: 2024 }, // chunk_count: 86, // external_id: null // } // Poll until ready while (document.status !== "ready" && document.status !== "failed") { await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 2000)); document = await ragie.documents.get({ documentId: document.id }); } ``` -------------------------------- ### Embedded Dropbox Connector - Configuration Options Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/dropbox-connection This JSON example shows the configuration body for an embedded Dropbox connector. It includes parameters like redirect_uri, partition, source_type, and importantly, 'account_mode' to specify either a 'team' or 'user' account. ```json { "redirect_uri": "https://secure.ragie.ai/connectors", "partition": "dropboxfiles", "source_type": "dropbox", "metadata": {}, "mode": "hi_res", "theme": "system", "page_limit": 1000, "config": { "account_mode": "team" } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Freshdesk Metadata Configuration (JSON) Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/freshdesk-connection Example of JSON format for associating metadata with Freshdesk data when creating a connection in Ragie AI. This metadata can be used for later filtering. It is optional and can be left blank. ```json { "company": "acme" } ``` -------------------------------- ### GET /instructions/{instruction_id}/entities Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/rate-limits Lists entities related to an instruction. Rate limits vary by plan. ```APIDOC ## GET /instructions/{instruction_id}/entities ### Description Lists entities related to an instruction. ### Method GET ### Endpoint /instructions/{instruction_id}/entities ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **instruction_id** (string) - Required - The ID of the instruction. #### Query Parameters None #### Request Body None ### Request Example ```json {} ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **entities** (array) - A list of entities related to the instruction. - **name** (string) - The name of the entity. - **type** (string) - The type of the entity. #### Response Example ```json { "entities": [ { "name": "Ragie AI", "type": "Organization" } ] } ``` ### Rate Limits - Developer: 10 / m - Starter: 500 / m - Pro: 1000 / m - Enterprise: Custom ``` -------------------------------- ### Create Authenticator Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/white-label-with-embed-flow This endpoint allows you to create an authenticator with your Google OAuth information. This is a prerequisite for initiating the OAuth connection. ```APIDOC ## POST /authenticators ### Description Creates a new authenticator entry with provided OAuth credentials for a specific provider. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /authenticators ### Parameters #### Request Body - **provider** (string) - Required - The OAuth provider (e.g., "google"). - **name** (string) - Required - A user-defined name for the authenticator. - **client_id** (string) - Required - The OAuth client ID obtained from the provider. - **client_secret** (string) - Required - The OAuth client secret obtained from the provider. - **project_number** (string) - Optional - The project number from the cloud provider (e.g., GCP). - **domain** (string) - Required - The authorized domain for the application. ### Request Example ```json { "provider": "google", "name": "Ragie Demo", "client_id": "client_id", "client_secret": "client_secret", "project_number": "project_number", "domain": "https://demo.ragie.ai" } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **provider** (string) - The OAuth provider. - **name** (string) - The name of the authenticator. - **id** (string) - The unique identifier for the created authenticator. #### Response Example ```json { "provider": "google", "name": "Ragie Demo", "id": "b5e77fb7-026c-408f-837a-2bf3b4ba0c6b" } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Initiate OAuth Connection Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/white-label-with-embed-flow This endpoint initiates the OAuth connection process, returning a URL that redirects the user to the provider's authentication and authorization flow. ```APIDOC ## POST /connections/oauth ### Description Initiates an OAuth connection for file selection with custom branding. Returns a URL to start the authentication flow. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /connections/oauth ### Parameters #### Request Body - **redirect_uri** (string) - Required - The redirect URL configured for the OAuth application. - **partition** (string) - Required - The data partition identifier (e.g., "google_files"). - **source_type** (string) - Required - The type of data source (e.g., "google_drive"). - **metadata** (object) - Optional - Additional metadata for the connection. - **mode** (string) - Optional - The connection mode (e.g., "hi_res"). - **theme** (string) - Optional - The UI theme to apply (e.g., "system"). - **page_limit** (integer) - Optional - The page limit for data retrieval. - **authenticator_id** (string) - Required - The ID of the pre-configured authenticator. ### Request Example ```json { "redirect_uri": "", "partition": "google_files", "source_type": "google_drive", "metadata": {}, "mode": "hi_res", "theme": "system", "page_limit": 100, "authenticator_id": "b5e77fb7-026c-408f-837a-2bf3b4ba0c6b" } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **url** (string) - The URL to redirect the user for OAuth authentication. #### Response Example ```json { "url": "https://connect.staging.ragie.ai/embed/connect?auth=some_long_string" } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### GET /documents/{document_id}/summary Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/rate-limits Retrieves a summary of a specific document. Rate limits vary by plan. ```APIDOC ## GET /documents/{document_id}/summary ### Description Retrieves a summary of a specific document. ### Method GET ### Endpoint /documents/{document_id}/summary ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **document_id** (string) - Required - The ID of the document. #### Query Parameters None #### Request Body None ### Request Example ```json {} ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **summary** (string) - The summary of the document. #### Response Example ```json { "summary": "This document discusses Ragie AI's API capabilities." } ``` ### Rate Limits - Developer: 10 / m - Starter: 500 / m - Pro: 1000 / m - Enterprise: Custom ``` -------------------------------- ### GET /documents/{document_id}/source Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/rate-limits Retrieves the source information for a specific document. Rate limits vary by plan. ```APIDOC ## GET /documents/{document_id}/source ### Description Retrieves the source information for a specific document. ### Method GET ### Endpoint /documents/{document_id}/source ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **document_id** (string) - Required - The ID of the document. #### Query Parameters None #### Request Body None ### Request Example ```json {} ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **source** (string) - The source of the document (e.g., URL, filename). #### Response Example ```json { "source": "http://example.com/document.pdf" } ``` ### Rate Limits - Developer: 10 / m - Starter: 500 / m - Pro: 1000 / m - Enterprise: Custom ``` -------------------------------- ### List Connections Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/managing-connections Retrieves a list of all the connections currently configured. This is often the first step to get connection IDs for further operations. ```APIDOC ## GET /connections ### Description See a list of all the connections you currently have. ### Method GET ### Endpoint https://api.ragie.ai/connections ### Parameters #### Path Parameters None #### Query Parameters None #### Request Body None ### Request Example ```bash curl https://api.ragie.ai/connections -H "Authorization: Bearer $RAGIE_API_KEY" ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **connections** (array) - A list of connection objects. #### Response Example ```json { "connections": [ { "id": "conn_123", "name": "My Connection", "enabled": true, "metadata": {"source": "google_drive"} } ] } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### GET /documents/{docId}/chunks/{chunkId}/content Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/changes-to-the-api Retrieves the raw content of a document chunk in a specified format, supporting streaming for audio and video. ```APIDOC ## Get Document Chunk Content ### Description Retrieves the content of a document chunk in the format specified by the `mediaType` parameter. This endpoint supports streaming media for audio and video documents. ### Method GET ### Endpoint `/documents/{docId}/chunks/{chunkId}/content` ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **docId** (string) - Required - The ID of the document. - **chunkId** (string) - Required - The ID of the chunk. #### Query Parameters - **mediaType** (string) - Required - The desired MIME type of the content (e.g., `audio/mpeg`, `text/plain-text`). - **download** (boolean) - Optional - If `true`, the content is returned as a file for download. Defaults to `false`. ### Request Example ```typescript const response = await client.documents.getChunkContent({ documentId: "docId", chunkId: "chunkId", mediaType: "audio/mpeg", }); ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - The response body will be the content of the chunk in the specified `mediaType`. For audio/video, if `download` is `false`, it's a stream; if `true`, it's a downloadable file. If `mediaType` is `application/json`, it behaves like the Get Document Chunk endpoint. #### Error Response - An error will occur if the requested `mediaType` is not supported for the chunk's document type. ``` -------------------------------- ### GET /documents/{docId}/chunks/{chunkId} Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/changes-to-the-api Retrieves a specific document chunk with detailed information, including word-level timestamps for audio and video modalities. ```APIDOC ## Get Document Chunk ### Description Retrieves a specific document chunk, providing granular data such as word-level timestamps for audio or video files. Includes metadata, links, and modality-specific data. ### Method GET ### Endpoint `/documents/{docId}/chunks/{chunkId}` ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **docId** (string) - Required - The ID of the document. - **chunkId** (string) - Required - The ID of the chunk. ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **id** (string) - The ID of the chunk. - **index** (integer) - The index of the chunk within the document. - **text** (string) - The text content of the chunk. - **metadata** (object) - Contains metadata like start and end times. - **start_time** (number) - The start time of the chunk. - **end_time** (number) - The end time of the chunk. - **links** (object) - Links to related resources (self, text content, document, audio streams, etc.). - **modality_data** (object or null) - Contains modality-specific data, such as word timestamps for audio. Returns null for static files. - **type** (string) - The modality type (e.g., "audio"). - **word_timestamps** (array) - An array of objects, each containing word, start time, end time, and probability. #### Response Example ```json { "id": "id123", "index": 0, "text": "The text of the chunk.", "metadata": { "end_time": 57.68, "start_time": 2.44 }, "links": { "self": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId", "type": "application/json" }, "self_text": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId/content?media_type=text/plain-text", "type": "text/plain-text" }, "document": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId", "type": "application/json" }, "document_text": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/content?media_type=text/plain-text", "type": "text/plain-text" }, "self_audio_stream": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg", "type": "audio/mpeg" }, "self_audio_download": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/chunks/chunkId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg&download=true", "type": "audio/mpeg" }, "document_audio_stream": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg", "type": "audio/mpeg" }, "document_audio_download": { "href": "https://api.ragie.ai/documents/docId/content?media_type=audio/mpeg&download=true", "type": "audio/mpeg" } }, "modality_data": { "type": "audio", "word_timestamps": [ { "start_time": 2.44, "end_time": 3.06, "word": " President", "probability": 0.75537109375 }, { "start_time": 3.06, "end_time": 3.38, "word": " Pitzer,", "probability": 0.67431640625 } ] } } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### GET /documents/{document_id} Source: https://docs.ragie.ai/docs/rate-limits Retrieves a specific document by its ID. Rate limits vary by plan. ```APIDOC ## GET /documents/{document_id} ### Description Retrieves a specific document by its ID. ### Method GET ### Endpoint /documents/{document_id} ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **document_id** (string) - Required - The ID of the document to retrieve. #### Query Parameters None #### Request Body None ### Request Example ```json {} ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **document_id** (string) - The ID of the document. - **content** (string) - The content of the document. #### Response Example ```json { "document_id": "doc_12345", "content": "This is the content of the document." } ``` ### Rate Limits - Developer: 1000 / m - Starter: 2000 / m - Pro: 4000 / m - Enterprise: Custom ```
